context: This is the heart of telling stories: saying what you did. You already know fui (I went) and vi (I saw) - now add a handful more "I did X" verbs and you can narrate almost any day.
explanation: When someone asks qué hiciste? (what did you do?), you answer with the "I" past form. Two you already have: fui = I went and estuve = I was / I stayed somewhere (la semana pasada estuve en Albir). Most other verbs are regular and predictable - that pattern comes in the next batches, one verb at a time.

examples.note: qué hiciste = what did you do - the question that starts every story.

examples.es: fui al gimnasio y me quedé una hora
examples.en: I went to the gym and stayed an hour
examples.note: fui = I went; me quedé = I stayed.

examples.es: comí en un bar y bebí una cerveza
examples.en: I ate at a bar and drank a beer
examples.note: comí (I ate) and bebí (I drank) - the -í ending for these verbs.

examples.es: compré ropa nueva y visité Altea
examples.en: I bought new clothes and visited Altea
examples.note: compré (I bought) and visité (I visited) - the -é ending.

examples.es: caminé al faro y subí la montaña
examples.en: I walked to the lighthouse and climbed the mountain
examples.note: caminé (I walked), subí (I went up / climbed).
